As a supervisor, Milk advocated for the rights of the LGBTQ+ community, including the repeal of laws that discriminated against gay people. He also worked to address issues such as housing, poverty, and environmental protection.

Tragically, Milk's life was cut short when he was assassinated on November 27, 1978, by Dan White, a fellow supervisor who had resigned from the board earlier that year. White's actions sparked the "White Night Riot," a protest that drew thousands of people to City Hall in San Francisco.
